trigger: User runs 'config vxlan add <name> <src_ip>' to configure a VXLAN tunnel endpoint

steps.action: User runs 'config vxlan add <vtep_name> <src_ip>'. The add_vxlan() click command validates the source IP and interface name, enforces single-VTEP policy, then writes VXLAN_TUNNEL entry to CONFIG_DB with field src_ip=<src_ip>.

Subscribed to CONFIG_DB CFG_VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE_NAME via Consumer. doTask() dispatches to doVxlanTunnelCreateTask(), which caches the tunnel's src_ip and writes the entry to APPL_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE using ProducerStateTable m_appVxlanTunnelTable.

When user subsequently runs 'config vxlan map add <vtep> <vlan> <vni>', doVxlanTunnelMapCreateTask() creates a per-VNI Linux VXLAN netdevice (<vtep>-<vlan_id>) via ip-link and bridge commands, then writes the map entry to APPL_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_MAP_TABLE.
steps.verify: ip -d link show type vxlan; redis-cli -n 0 KEYS 'VXLAN_TUNNEL_MAP_TABLE:*'

steps.action: ConsumerStateTable on APPL_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE fires addOperation(). A VxlanTunnel object is constructed in-memory (tunnel not yet in hardware at this stage — hardware creation is deferred until a map entry arrives).
steps.output: VxlanTunnel object created in vxlan_tunnel_table_ map; tunnel not yet in ASIC_DB
steps.verify: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; grep 'Vxlan tunnel.*was added' /var/log/swss/orchagent.log

steps.action: ConsumerStateTable on APPL_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_MAP_TABLE fires addOperation(). On first map entry for this tunnel, createTunnelHw() is called which: (a) creates SAI tunnel map objects (SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_MAP) for VNI-to-VLAN and VLAN-to-VNI via create_tunnel_map(); (b) creates the SAI tunnel object (SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL, type VXLAN, encap src IP) via create_tunnel(); (c) creates the tunnel termination entry (SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_TERM_TABLE_ENTRY, P2MP) via create_tunnel_termination(). Then creates the VNI-to-VLAN map entry (SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_MAP_ENTRY) via create_tunnel_map_entry(). All of these are written to ASIC_DB.
steps.verify: redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL*'; redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_TERM_TABLE_ENTRY*'; redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_MAP*'

steps.action: Reads ASIC_DB create notifications for SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_MAP, SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL, SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_TERM_TABLE_ENTRY, and SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL_MAP_ENTRY via processSingleEvent() -> processQuadEvent(). Calls vendor SAI library sai_tunnel_api->create_tunnel_map(), create_tunnel(), create_tunnel_term_table_entry(), and create_tunnel_map_entry() to program the hardware VTEP and VNI decap/encap tables.
steps.output: VTEP and VNI-to-VLAN decap/encap programmed in hardware
steps.verify: show vxlan tunnel; show vxlan vlanvnimap; redis-cli -n 1 HGETALL 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL:<oid>'

failure_injection_points.symptom: VXLAN_TUNNEL entry in CONFIG_DB but no corresponding entry in APPL_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check vxlanmgrd: docker exec swss supervisorctl status vxlanmgrd; check /var/log/swss/vxlanmgrd.log for errors; verify swss container is healthy

failure_injection_points.failure: vxlanmgrd cannot create Linux VXLAN netdevice for tunnel map
failure_injection_points.symptom: VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE written to APPL_DB but VXLAN_TUNNEL_MAP_TABLE missing; ip link shows no vxlan interfaces
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check vxlanmgrd log for 'Vxlan Net Dev creation failure'; verify VLAN exists before mapping (show vlan brief); check kernel module: lsmod | grep vxlan

failure_injection_points.symptom: VXLAN_TUNNEL_TABLE present in APPL_DB but no SAI objects in ASIC_DB; show vxlan tunnel shows no tunnels
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; grep 'vxlan tunnel' /var/log/swss/orchagent.log; verify orchagent is subscribed to APPL_DB

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent fails to create SAI tunnel hardware objects on map entry
failure_injection_points.symptom: Tunnel in APPL_DB and orchagent in-memory state but no ASIC_DB SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL entries; map write triggers SAI error
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent log for 'Can't create a tunnel object' or 'Can't create tunnel map'; verify underlay RIF (gUnderlayIfId) is initialized; check VLAN state: redis-cli -n 6 HGETALL 'VLAN_TABLE|Vlan<id>'

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd SAI vendor library returns error on tunnel create
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_TUNNEL entry present but syncd logs SAI_STATUS_FAILURE; VTEP not forwarding traffic
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check syncd: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; grep SAI_STATUS /var/log/syncd/syncd.log; check if ASIC supports P2MP mode: redis-cli -n 1 HGETALL 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_SWITCH:oid:*'
